LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA FUENTES' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES. THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED; PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. TO PRODUCE A PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE FOR DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS SET FORTH IN THE REPORT; AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $18,000. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES ARE DUE ON JAN-21-2020 AND THE PMK DEPOSITION SHALL TAKE PLACE NO LATER THAN FEB-18-2020. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE JORDAN CSR#12262, EMAIL: CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG

DISCOVERY 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

Defendant Lyft Incs Amended Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiffs Deposition Notices Issued To Two Lyft Employees In Their Individual Capacities
Off Calendar Jun-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mike Gosling, Esq., Fox Law, APC, 858-256-7616, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Steffanie A. Malla, Esq., Lewis Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith, 415-438-6670, appearing for Defendant Lyft Inc.; (CourtCall)
No other appearance.

All parties present stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo hearing today's motion.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. Amended Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiffs Deposition Notices Issued To Two Lyft Employees In Their Individual Capacities

GRANTED as to Ms.Lutz and DENIED as to Mr. Dorton. Deposition to take place on a date no later than July 17, 2020. A Discovery Referee is to be present and fees are to be split by parties. If trial date is continued, the deposition is to take place no later than 90 days before trial. Defendants counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and approved as to form to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christopher Hendricks, Esq. & Joanna L. Fox, Esq. (on Court Call) of FOX LAW APC, T:858.256.7616 for plaintiff/moving party;
Stephanie Malla, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS, T:415.262.8583, for defendant Lyft, Inc.;
Laura Grealish, Esq. of MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, T:415.788.1900, for defendant Shokur.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA FUENTES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is granted; Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant Lyft, Inc. to produce a person most knowledgeable for deposition is granted in part and denied in part as set forth in the report; and Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $18,000.

The Court further orders that Further Responses to form interrogatories are due on Jan-21-2020 and the PMK deposition shall take place no later than Feb-18-2020.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org
